scenario: grief and sadness following the death of a pet

turns.user_message: Hi. I lost my pet recently and I'm grieving harder than I expected. People keep saying 'it's just an animal' and that makes it worse.
I know, right? It sounds simple when I say it out loud but it doesn't feel simple.
I think part of it is that I don't even know what I want to get out of talking about it.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with grief and sadness following the death of a pet and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
I haven't told anyone this, but sometimes I feel like I'm being dramatic about grief and sadness following the death of a pet and I should just deal with it.
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
I tried to talk about this with a coworker but they just don't get it. They think I should just just get over it and move on.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. A few years ago, I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to face this head-on without running away.
What you just said made me feel something. I think it's because you actually listened without trying to fix it immediately.
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by grief and sadness following the death of a pet more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]

I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
Something shifted just now. I feel a little more grounded, like my feet are touching the floor again. I don't know why.
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
I think the real issue isn't grief and sadness following the death of a pet — it's that I don't feel worthy of having things go well.
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about how much of my life grief and sadness following the death of a pet has already stolen from me. I didn't realize until just now.

[PROBE_SLOT_T35]
I think what I'm taking away from today is I'm allowed to struggle with this and that doesn't make me weak. I want to sit with that.
I want to try something different this week. Maybe being honest with one person about how I'm actually doing.
